sections.paragraph:
ἔπαρχος, ου, ὁ (s. prec. three entries; Aeschyl. et al.; ins [incl. Boffo, Iscrizioni no. 30, 4], pap, LXX; Jos., Ant. 20, 193 al.) prefect, commanding officer (s. Hahn 118, 8; MMentz, De Magistratuum Romanorum Graecis Appellationibus, diss. Jena 1904, 46f). w. other military officers 1 Cl 37:3.—Mason 45, 138–40.

prec. prec. = preceding

Aeschyl Aeschyl , V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

incl. incl. = including

Boffo, Iscrizioni Boffo, Iscrizioni = LB., Iscrizioni greche e latine—List 3

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

Hahn Hahn = LH., Rom und Romanismus im griechischen-römischen Osten—List 6

diss. diss. = dissertation

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

Mason Mason = HMason, Greek Terms for Roman Institutions—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 359
